sample.title: Why Space Companies Are Betting Big on Texas
sample.description:
Already home to SpaceX, Blue Origin and recent moon-landers Firefly Aerospace and Intuitive Machines, Texas is quickly becoming the center of the commercial space universe. Through a newly-formed agency, Texas officials have been doling out grants for space-related research, projects and emerging technology. So what does this mean for the "Lone Star State?" And why has it become such a proving ground for the startups of the future?

(Corrects map of Texas throughout video.)

SpaceX’s IPO to Mint Millionaires in Poor Texas Border Town: https://bloom.bg/4bsvut7

The company’s spaceport put Brownsville on the map, but locals fear being priced out by newly rich neighbors. 

00:00 How the space sector is changing 
01:08 Firefly Aerospace sees success in Texas 
02:24 Why Texas appeals to the space industry
04:48 SpaceX history in Texas 
06:10 How the state of Texas is now wooing space business
07:08 The changing relationship between industry and NASA
09:45 The SpaceX business model that paved the way

Watch more short documentaries: https://bloom.bg/4wWlMsa

sample.title: Why Gen Z Is Gambling With Its Future
sample.description:
With homeownership out of reach, artificial intelligence cutting job prospects and index funds feeling too slow, many young investors are turning to meme stocks, options and crypto to meet their financial goals.

sample.title: Why Prediction Markets Are So Hard To Regulate
sample.description:
Event contracts let people bet on outcomes ranging from sports and inflation to company milestones. But as these markets move closer to publicly traded businesses, old financial rules are being tested in new ways. CNBC's Ananya Chetia and Davis Giangiulio report on what this uncertainty could mean for platforms like Kalshi, Polymarket and others.

Credits:
Produced and shot by Charlotte Morabito
Reporting by Ananya Chetia, Davis Giangiulio
Edited by Andrea Miller
Animation by Alisa Stern, Jason Reginato
Senior Managing Producer - Shawn Baldwin
Additional Footage - Getty Images

0:00 Introduction
1:59 CFTC vs. SEC
4:27 Working together

sample.title: Elon Musk’s Memphis Takeover — The Marvels And Messiness Of SpaceX’s AI Buildout
sample.description:
Elon Musk's xAI, now known as SpaceXAI following a merger with SpaceX, has made the Memphis, Tennessee area the center of its AI ambitions. XAI is building three data centers there, as well as a power plant in Southaven, Mississippi. XAI's AI infrastructure there truly deserves its name Colossus. It's a modern marvel that was built with breakneck speed. Anthropic and Google are now renting compute capacity at the data centers in a deals worth billions of dollars. But some say the buildout has been chaotic. XAI is now facing multiple lawsuits accusing the company of using loud, pollution-emitting gas turbines, some without the required Clean Air Act permits. CNBC traveled to Memphis and Southaven to speak with locals to learn why the data centers are such a flashpoint, to find out why backlash against data centers is spreading nationwide, and to learn what hyperscalers can do to win support as they race to build AI infrastructure.

Chapters:
00:00 - 3:54 Introduction
3:55 - 10:28 SpaceX’s AI ambitions
10:29 - 17:02 Gas-burning turbines
17:03 - 20:28 Constant noise
20:29 - 22:37 Millions of gallons of water
22:38 - 28:59 Growing AI backlash

sample.title: Consumer Prices See Biggest Drop In Six Years In June
sample.description:
The June consumer price index shows that annual inflation is moving closer to the Federal Reserve’s 2% target. Energy put downward pressure on inflation as a lull in the Iran war easing prices for consumers. CNBC’s Alex Harring breaks down what the June CPI reveals about prices.

sample.title: How China came to dominate the global EV factory boom
sample.description:
Chinese automakers have made over $100 billion in EV and battery investments abroad since 2019, according to Atlas Public Policy, far outpacing US firms. CNBC’s Robert Ferris has more on how the Asian country got here and why.

sample.title: Senator says he won't vote for Blanche until he meets with Epstein victims
sample.description:
Republican Senator Thom Tillis, a key vote on the Senate Judiciary Committee, said he would not vote to advance President Trump's nominee for attorney general unless the nominee, Todd Blanche, meets ‌with victims of Jeffrey Epstein and their lawyers.
 
Blanche likely cannot advance without Tillis' vote.

sample.title: World Cup's 'Chief Watchers' savour tournament's dream job
sample.description:
World Cup fanatics Kevin Akoto and Austin Franklin have lived the dream of any diehard football fan this year: watching every match of the ‌tournament from start to finish — and getting paid for it.
 
To experience the spectacle firsthand, Jenna Zucker stepped inside a glass-walled viewing cube in Times Square on matchday. The setup feels a little like life in a fishbowl, drawing curious onlookers who stop to peek inside or watch the televisions through the window.
 
#FIFAWorldCup #Soccer #Football #WorldCup #FIFA

sample.title: The new replacement bridge has already been built and will be slide into place. #BBCNews
What could Andy Burnham's plan be in Downing Street? | BBC Newscast
sample.description:
Adam is joined by broadcaster Andrew Marr, who is one of the very few people to have interviewed Andy Burnham before he goes into number 10.

As Burnham prepares to take the reins of government, some things remain unclear about what he will do when he gets there. Who will be in his cabinet? And how will he tackle the economic challenges that faced Keir Starmer’s premiership?

sample.title: Would you drive around with this licence plate? #BBCNews
Who is Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine's drone war architect sacked as defence minister? | BBC News
sample.description:
Protests have been taking place in several Ukrainian cities against President Volodymyr Zelensky's surprise dismissal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

A tech savvy, young, ambitious minister, Mr Fedorov has become the face of Ukraine's hugely successful drone warfare campaign. But did he push too hard, and clash with the more traditional heads of the military? 

We answer all your questions on who he is, why he was removed and what it all means on the battlefield. 

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#Ukraine  #BBCNews

sample.title: What I found travelling through the World Cup's US cities | BBC News
sample.description:
In many countries, football is a lifelong obsession. In the United States, not so much.
 
Throughout the tournament there has been plenty of debate around ticket prices, extreme heat and whether American fans have truly embraced it.
 
BBC Sport's Sam Harris travelled through New York, Philadelphia, Boston and Houston to find out what the FIFA World Cup experience in the USA is really like.
